name: If the machine that crushed my hand was defective, can I sue the manufacturer?
description: Yes. While workers\' comp covers your medical bills, you can often file a separate third-party lawsuit against a manufacturer for a defective machine to recover

mainEntity.name: If the machine that crushed my hand was defective, can I sue the manufacturer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. While workers\' comp covers your medical bills, you can often file a separate third-party lawsuit against a manufacturer for a defective machine to recover additional damages like pain and suffering.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If a machine malfunctions due to a design or manufacturing flaw, you may have two separate legal paths: a workers\' compensation claim against your employer and a "third-party" product liability lawsuit against the machine manufacturer. Workers\' comp covers medical bills and a portion of lost wages regardless of fault, while a lawsuit against a manufacturer can recover "pain and suffering" damages not available through comp.

This is a critical distinction because workers\' comp often fails to cover the full scope of a life-altering injury. A third-party claim allows you to hold the maker of the dangerous equipment accountable for their negligence.

Key factors include:
- The maintenance history of the machine.
- Any previous recalls or known defects with that model.
- Whether the machine’s safety guards were improperly designed.
- Expert analysis of the mechanical failure.

Preserving the machine in its post-accident state is essential for a product liability claim. Consulting a legal professional early ensures that independent experts can inspect the equipment before it is altered or destroyed.
